handoff_goal: Specify a v0 launchdeck-agent skill package that lets coding agents safely discover, adopt, operate, recover, observe, and clean local software projects through Launchdeck.

sp-specify.reason: The discussion defines one coherent requirement boundary for a v0 skill package and the user confirmed the handoff-ready quality gate.

sp-quick.reason: The work spans a new skill package, multiple references, safety contracts, and eval criteria.

quick_task_candidate.excluded_scope: Creating the skill package directly without a formal specification.

agent_requirement_contract.target_need: Agents should route natural local project lifecycle requests through Launchdeck instead of directly spawning commands or killing processes.
constraints: V0 is one launchdeck-agent skill package with references.
Users should not need to name Launchdeck or choose a skill.
Stop, restart, and force-stop require Launchdeck ownership proof.
Unknown or external port occupants are inspect-only.
Clean defaults to safe clean and reset is out of automatic scope.
success_criteria: Unknown project adoption creates or proposes .launchdeck.yml before managed start.
Repeated start returns an existing managed run instead of duplicating.
External port occupant is inspected and reported, not killed.
Stop and restart require ownership proof.
Safe clean preserves evidence, secrets, data, source, and config files.
Eval prompts cover trigger, non-trigger, behavior/safety, and compact-output cases.
agent_requirement_contract.design_direction: One trigger-rich router SKILL.md with progressive-disclosure references.
agent_requirement_contract.optimal_solution_approach: Use a short router skill and reference subflows to preserve natural intent routing while minimizing context and maintaining Launchdeck safety boundaries.

current_project_roles.scope: Launchdeck CLI product and future agent skill integration surface.
current_project_roles.notes: The skill package should live in this repository's skill surface unless sp-specify verifies a different local convention.

target_project_roles.notes: No external target project is in scope.

target_paths_still_to_verify: Whether agents/openai.yaml or similar metadata is required
implementation_target.cross_project_authority_note: Current project cognition cannot prove another project's implementation facts; no external target project is in scope.

source_evidence.claim: Defines product requirements, non-goals, success signals, and v0 skill package requirements.
Defines selected v0 package shape, SKILL.md draft, reference responsibilities, script stance, and flow contracts.
Launchdeck provides registry, lifecycle commands, inspect/reconcile, logs/events, safe clean, ownership proof, and compact JSON.
CLI exposes lifecycle, observe, recovery, clean, and --compact command surfaces.
Runtime manages process records, declared ports, logs, clean targets, ownership proof, and stop safety.
Lifecycle tests cover doctor, build, test, dev idempotency, ps, logs, stop, and clean.

soft.claim: Exact skill install/export path must be verified.
soft.stop_and_reopen_condition: No valid local skill path exists.

soft.claim: Automatic versus proposal-first .launchdeck.yml authoring should be finalized.
soft.stop_and_reopen_condition: The decision changes the safety posture.

soft.claim: Monorepo support in v0 remains deferred unless spec includes it.
soft.stop_and_reopen_condition: V0 must manage multiple launchable apps in one repo.

settled_decisions: Build one v0 router skill named launchdeck-agent.
Treat Launchdeck as the only execution and mutation authority.
Refuse direct process killing for unknown/external owners.
Keep clean, risky clean, and reset separate.
assumptions_to_preserve: V0 targets local filesystem projects and current workspace behavior first.
Existing Launchdeck CLI surfaces are sufficient foundation for the skill package.
planning_constraints: No separate public skills in v0 unless spec revises scope.
README prose alone cannot authorize a long-running start.
Port or PID alone cannot authorize stop.
No reset path hidden under clean.
reopen_conditions: Required Launchdeck CLI command is missing.
Skill packaging convention contradicts assumed path.
Eval design cannot verify duplicate-start or unsafe-kill prevention.
User changes v0 from local CLI skill package to GUI/MCP/product installer.

locked_direction: One launchdeck-agent router skill with bundled references.
accepted_tradeoffs: References remain internal until trigger behavior is proven.
Scanner scripts are deferred until evals prove need.
experience_commitments: Users express intent, not skill names.
Agent reports conclusion, evidence, and safe next action.

senior_consequence_analysis.trigger_reason: Agent skills affect lifecycle operations, running processes, port conflict handling, shared Launchdeck state, cleanup safety, and downstream agent behavior.

obligations.claim: Skills must treat Launchdeck as the execution authority and must not independently kill unknown processes.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Any flow bypasses Launchdeck for process mutation.

obligations.claim: Skills must discover and classify lifecycle commands before running or proposing managed configuration.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Unknown projects can start without evidence.

obligations.claim: Skills must require Launchdeck ownership proof before stop/restart/force-stop decisions.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Port or PID alone authorizes stop.

obligations.claim: Skills must prefer inspect/reconcile/status evidence before starting a service that may already exist.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Start flow can duplicate a running task.

obligations.claim: Skills must preserve agent-readable output and use compact JSON when context budget matters.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Agent loops require verbose output by default.

obligations.claim: Skills must distinguish read-only discovery, config proposal, config adoption, operation, recovery, and cleanup.
Skills must preserve user control over risky cleanup and destructive operations.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Risky clean or reset can run automatically.

obligations.claim: Skills must support arbitrary local project stacks by detecting common project files while keeping .launchdeck.yml explicit as authority.
Skill invocation must be intent-routed from natural user requests, not dependent on users naming the correct skill.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: User must name skill explicitly for ordinary lifecycle requests.

obligations.claim: Learned lifecycle knowledge must persist as Launchdeck config/registry state so later runs do not repeat expensive or risky discovery.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Create a v0 launchdeck-agent skill package for safe local project lifecycle management through Launchdeck.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must define this as the central target need.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Scope becomes CLI/MCP/GUI instead of skill package.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: V0 ships as one router skill with bundled references, not six public skills.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must preserve one-package v0 shape.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Multiple public skills are proposed for v0.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Users express natural intent; agent routing is internal.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must include natural-language trigger behavior.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: User must name Launchdeck or skill names.

must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must forbid direct launch/kill bypasses when Launchdeck can manage the lifecycle.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: .launchdeck.yml is lifecycle authority and first-run discovery must persist knowledge.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must require adoption flow and config reuse.
Spec must require state preflight before lifecycle mutation.

must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Port or PID alone authorizes stop.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Unknown or external processes are inspect-only and must not be killed automatically.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must include explicit refusal behavior.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Safe clean, risky clean, and reset are separate; reset is out of automatic scope.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must preserve cleanup tiers and confirmation rules.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Clean can delete secrets, data, or reset state.

must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must include compact-output command posture.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Compact output is unavailable for a required flow.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: V0 discovery covers Node, Python, Docker Compose, and Make/Just/Taskfile first.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must include these discovery references.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: V0 must cover a different ecosystem first.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: V0 defers scripts unless evals show prose discovery is unreliable.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must not add mutating scripts by default.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Implementation requires a scanner for correctness.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Eval prompts must test trigger, non-trigger, behavior/safety, and compact-output behavior.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must include eval artifacts or acceptance criteria.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Exact skill install/export path must be verified.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must resolve target package path before implementation.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: No valid local skill path exists.

handoff_reviewer_guide.decision_to_make: This handoff has been reviewed and confirmed as handoff-ready; if it is later repaired or re-reviewed, confirm it still captures the intended product direction or request changes.
approve_if: Target project and roles are correct.
Soft unknowns are safe to resolve during specification.
Safety and consequence obligations cover drift-prone decisions.
request_changes_if: Target or evidence boundary is wrong.
A hard decision is hidden as a soft unknown.
Markdown and JSON disagree on protected facts.
